command.text: find /workspace -type f -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk 'NR==1{h=$1; files=$2; next} $1==h{files=files"\n"$2} $1!=h{if(NF>1){print files} h=$1; files=$2} END{if(NF>1){print files}}'

raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that lists files with identical content in /workspace. Use find, md5sum, sort, uniq -d? Approach: find /workspace -type f -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | uniq -d -w32? But need paths of duplicates. Use awk to group. Command: find ... | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk '...'. Let's produce one line: find /workspace -type f -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk '{print $1, $2}'? Actually md5sum outputs hash and filename. We want groups with same hash. Use awk to collect. One-liner: find /workspace -type f -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk 'NR==1{h=$1; files=$2; next} $1==h{files=files"\n"$2} $1!=h{if(NF>1){print h; print files} h=$1; files=$2} END{if(NF>1){print h; print files}}' This prints hash and files. But requirement: list all files with their paths that have identical content. So output file paths only. Could output each group separated. Provide command. Let's output just paths: find ... | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk '...'. We'll output each file path per line but duplicates groups maybe. Simpler: find ... | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk 'NR==1{h=$1; files=$2; next} $1==h{files=files"\n"$2} $1!=h{if(NF>1){print files} h=$1; files=$2} END{if(NF>1){print files}}' This prints groups separated by blank line? We'll just output each file path per line but duplicates groups separated by newline. Provide command.
